dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: How two of a Product's Solutions — and so two repositories — depend on one another. Distinct from WorkItemLink, which joins two pieces of work: this joins two systems, outlives any sprint, and answers 'if we change the API, what else moves?'

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data.

entries.rules: must reference a Solution row; must not equal toSolutionId; must share a Product with it
entries.description: The Solution that depends on the other

entries.rules: one of: callsApi / sharesSchema / publishesEvent / buildsOn
entries.description: How they depend on each other. 'buildsOn' means one is compiled or deployed against the other — it orders work, so it must stay acyclic. The rest describe runtime, where mutual dependence is a real and workable arrangement
Why the dependency exists, in the developer's own words

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Until the link is removed or either Solution is deleted. Deleting a Solution removes every link touching it — a link to a deleted Solution is not a dependency, it is a dangling row.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields that need extra protection?

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.answer: Both Solutions exist, differ, and belong to the same Product — this map is drawn per Product, so a link reaching outside it would be invisible from both ends. 'buildsOn' must never form a cycle, directly or transitively, because neither side could be built first. The other kinds may loop freely: two services calling each other's APIs is a real arrangement, and refusing it would make the map lie about the system it describes.

changesOverTime.guidance: Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change.

rounds.change: New: Developer Planning needs a cross-repo map, so a change in one repository can be traced to everything it reaches.
